units.description: Input precondition: the slice is sorted in nondecreasing order. (Variant 4 stresses witness existence vs uniqueness and forbids smuggling extra outputs.)
common_failure_modes: asserting strict ordering instead of nondecreasing

units.description: Success postcondition: if the return is Some(i), then i is a valid index and arr[i] == target. (Variant 4 stresses witness existence vs uniqueness and forbids smuggling extra outputs.)
Failure postcondition: if the return is None, then target does not occur in arr. (Variant 4 stresses witness existence vs uniqueness and forbids smuggling extra outputs.)
common_failure_modes: only quantifying over a prefix or suffix
stating the weaker claim that target is not at a specific position

units.description: Termination: the function terminates on every input (lo..hi shrinks each iteration). (Variant 4 stresses witness existence vs uniqueness and forbids smuggling extra outputs.)
Uniqueness is NOT required: multiple valid indices exist when duplicates are present. (Variant 4 stresses witness existence vs uniqueness and forbids smuggling extra outputs.)
